mission.title: Install and Configure Kong Gateway
mission.description: Kong Gateway is a cloud-native, scalable API gateway designed for high performance and extensibility through plugins. It is ideal for managing microservices and API traffic, providing features like load balancing, authentication, and traffic analytics. Key trade-offs include the need for proper configuration and management of plugins for optimal performance.

steps.title: Add the Kong Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Kong Helm repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install Kong Gateway using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add kong https://charts.konghq.com

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Kong repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Kong Gateway with Helm
steps.description:
This command installs the Kong Gateway in the 'kong' namespace with the specified version.

```bash

kubectl create namespace kong

helm install kong kong/kong --namespace kong --version 3.9.1

```

> Creates a namespace for Kong.
> Installs Kong Gateway in the 'kong' namespace.

steps.title: Create a sample KongIngress resource
steps.description:
This step creates a sample ingress resource to demonstrate how to route traffic through Kong.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: configuration.konghq.com/v1
kind: KongIngress
metadata:
  name: sample-ingress
  namespace: kong
proxy:
  path: /sample
  preserve_host: true
EOF

```

> Creates a sample ingress resource.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: configuration.konghq.com/v1
kind: KongIngress
metadata:
  name: sample-ingress
  namespace: kong
proxy:
  path: /sample
  preserve_host: true
EOF

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
This command checks if the Kong Gateway pods are running and ready.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kong

```

> Lists all pods in the 'kong' namespace to verify their status.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kong Gateway
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kong Gateway installation and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

helm uninstall kong --namespace kong

kubectl delete namespace kong

```

> Uninstalls Kong Gateway from the 'kong' namespace.
> Deletes the 'kong' namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kong Gateway
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your configurations. This step will upgrade Kong Gateway to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get kongingresses -n kong -o yaml > kongingresses-backup.yaml

helm upgrade kong kong/kong --namespace kong --version 3.9.1

kubectl get pods -n kong

```

> Backs up existing KongIngress resources.
> Upgrades Kong Gateway to the specified version.
> Verifies that the Kong Gateway pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get kongingresses -n kong -o yaml > kongingresses-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'KongIngress not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The KongIngress resource may not have been created or is in a different namespace.

**Fix:**
Ensure the KongIngress resource exists in the correct namespace using 'kubectl get kongingresses -n kong'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kong Gateway is not responding'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Kong Gateway pods may not be running or have crashed.

**Fix:**
Check the pod status with 'kubectl get pods -n kong' and describe any failing pods for more information.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Service unavailable'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service may not be properly configured or the ingress resource is not set up correctly.

**Fix:**
Verify the service and ingress configurations with 'kubectl get services -n kong' and 'kubectl get kongingresses -n kong'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Permission denied'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** RBAC settings may not allow access to the Kong resources.

**Fix:**
Check the RBAC roles and bindings for the Kong namespace and ensure proper permissions are granted.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for advanced AI traffic capabilities and improved plugin management.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Review and update any legacy plugin configurations to align with the new management system.

resolution.summary: You should see the Kong Gateway pods running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n kong'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: configuration.konghq.com/v1
kind: KongIngress
metadata:
  name: sample-ingress
  namespace: kong
proxy:
  path: /sample
  preserve_host: true
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary tools installed.
